src: Well you'll see that in that apology Milton appears to be conscious of the very point that I am trying to make, that is to say it might be considered out of place in this prose work to speak of myself in direct factual terms, although a poet — a poet intending to write of things unattempted yet in prose or rhyme — a poet soaring in the high region of his fancies, with his garland and singing robes about him, in other words where we can't or aren't really invited to make out his individual identity very clearly because it is his role as poet that concerns us, there he clearly feels it would be proper.
mt: Bueno, verás que en esa disculpa, Milton parece estar consciente del mismo punto que yo estoy tratando de hacer, es decir, que podría considerarse fuera de lugar en este trabajo en prosa hablar de mí mismo en términos fácticos directos, aunque un poeta, un poeta que tenga la intención de escribir sobre cosas que aún no se han intentado en prosa o en verso, un poeta que se eleva en la alta región de sus fantasías, con su corona y sus ropas de canto a su alrededor, en otras palabras, donde no podemos o no estamos realmente invitados a distinguir su identidad individual con mucha claridad porque es su rol como poeta lo que nos concierne, allí él claramente siente que sería apropiado.

src: For an annual sum of £100 he was to take on the care, culture and management of the Garden for a term of seven years from Michaelmas, to keep in repair the stove, greenhouse and other buildings and utensils contained in them, to make a catalogue of the plants and, an additional commercial perquisite, to be allowed to sell surplus fruit and plants for his own benefit.
mt: Por una suma anual de 100 £, él se encargaría del cuidado, cultivo y administración del Jardín por un período de siete años a partir de Michaelmas, manteniendo en buen estado la estufa, el invernadero y los demás edificios y utensilios que contenían, haciendo un catálogo de las plantas y, como prerrogativa comercial adicional, se le permitiría vender el excedente de frutas y plantas para su propio beneficio.

src: And if you have once demonstrated you skill at copying chairs, you can get on with meeting all the requests you will surely have from family and friends.

src: This is how the romantic-lyric elements in Elvis's style, for example, could take on new meanings, mediated as they were by country and western's and black music's previous appropriations of them, by the ‘under view’ of the working class of the South on the American Dream, and by an awareness of a new freedom in adolescent leisure behaviour.
mt: In questo modo, ad esempio, gli elementi romantico-lirici nello stile di Elvis potevano assumere nuovi significati, mediati come erano dalle precedenti appropriazioni di country e western e della musica nera, dalla "visione inferiore" della classe operaia del Sud sul sogno americano e dalla consapevolezza di una nuova libertà nel comportamento ricreativo degli adolescenti.

src: ‘He was last year about now but Easter's late, I hadn't thought on, and he'll not move down until Palm Sunday like as not, and if he's still over on the mountain he'll be up Three Valleys Pass and that's an hour and a half of a walk for him, going as the crow flies, but longer for us in the jeep because there's no direct road — and then to get at him we'd have to take a cart track that'll be more like a river bed after yesterday's rain.
mt: “Él estaba aquí el año pasado, pero la Pascua llegó tarde, no lo había pensado, y no bajará hasta el Domingo de Ramos, y si aún está en la montaña, estará en el paso de los Tres Valles y eso es una hora y media de caminata para él, si se va en línea recta, pero más para nosotros en el jeep porque no hay una carretera directa, y luego, para llegar a él, tendríamos que tomar una pista de carretas que será más como un lecho de río después de la lluvia de ayer.

src: Well we were lucky in that we've been able to do, have a, a very good relationship with a company called well known in the marine side and they put in forty five thousand pounds into er the scheme and promised that before Christmas and that was reading the paper one day in November the, the Robert the National Heritage Minister saying that they may be, may, if you're lucky, going to put some money into sport and er so we contacted them and we were one of the first sports to get, had money doubled as they say in the bingo hall, so we er we now have ninety, ninety thousand pounds and which I wh has been distributed or will be distributed in the, in the following way so that's how we're gonna spend it and er these er, the administration represent we were basically overwhelmed with enquiries and s we took on a person in order to, to do it, the normal R Y A staff had already got enough on so we took on a girl called Sara who answers all the queries on the Year of Youth Hotline and erm we are also running the boat shows, the four or five N B L challenge which is the flagship event for our sponsors which is er I won't go into the, the details but is a, a talent fight, talent spotting event for under sixteen year olds around the country and it provided fleet of dinghies, the prize for which is a dinghy which is not, not a bad prize I think you'll agree.
mt: Bueno, tuvimos suerte de que pudimos tener una relación muy buena con una empresa llamada, muy conocida en el ámbito marítimo, y pusieron cuarenta y cinco mil libras en el esquema y prometieron que antes de Navidad, y eso fue leyendo el periódico un día de noviembre, el Robert, el Ministro de Patrimonio Nacional, diciendo que podrían, podrían, si tienes suerte, poner algo de dinero en el deporte y, entonces contactamos con ellos y fuimos uno de los primeros deportes en conseguir, tener el dinero duplicado, como dicen en la sala de bingo, así que ahora tenemos noventa, noventa mil libras y que yo wh ha sido distribuido o se distribuirá de la siguiente manera, así es como lo vamos a gastar y er estos er, la administración representa que básicamente nos abrumarón con consultas y s contratamos a una persona para que lo haga, el personal normal de R Y A ya tenía suficiente, así que contratamos a una chica llamada Sara, que contesta todas las consultas en la línea directa del Año de la Juventud y, también estamos organizando los espectáculos de barcos, el desafío de cuatro o cinco N B L, que es el evento insignia para nuestros patrocinadores, que es, no entraré en los detalles, pero es una, una lucha de talentos, un evento de búsqueda de talentos para menores de dieciséis años en todo el país y se les proporciona una flota de barcos, cuyo premio es un barco, que no es un mal premio, creo que estaréis de acuerdo.

src: The loans are fixed until April 1990 to get over what was at the beginning of the week ‘the current period of uncertainty’ and has now become a gloomy fact.

mt: Il desiderio di dare una lezione agli esperti ha assunto proporzioni maniacali.

mt: De todos modos, pasé este complicado intervalo, y hasta el mar cooperó por una vez, llegando justo después de la explosión y barriendo cualquier rastro revelador que pudiera haber dejado una hora o más antes de que Diggs llegara desde el pueblo para inspeccionar la escena.

src: I think talking to them helped me a lot to get over those first few months.

src: Noreen was kept on after the try-out week, but she knew that was only on the strength of Fred, the Stage Manager's sympathetic report, and for the fact that she got on well with everyone in the company, especially Bernie.
mt: Noreen siguió después de la semana de prueba, pero sabía que era solo gracias a Fred, el informe simpático del director de escena, y por el hecho de que ella se llevaba bien con todos en la compañía, especialmente con Bernie.

src: A defendant whose doctor alleges that the plaintiff is malingering must be prepared to call that doctor at trial to make out the allegation and to face cross-examination.
mt: Un convenuto che sostiene, tramite il proprio medico, che il querelante stia fingendo la malattia deve essere pronto a chiamare il medico in giudizio per provare la sua affermazione e a sottoporsi a un controinterrogatorio.

src: ‘It would be marvellous if The Committee were to win the National,’ Scott said, ‘for the two owners have been immensely patient and have let me get on with bringing the horse back to his best after he had been off the course for two and a half years.’
mt: “Sería maravilloso si The Committee ganara el National”, dijo Scott, “porque los dos propietarios han sido inmensamente pacientes y me han dejado seguir adelante para llevar al caballo de nuevo a su mejor nivel después de que hubiera estado fuera del campo durante dos años y medio”.

src: Kiss is about as valid as a football game; it's a release, a chance for people to forget about their problems for a couple of hours, get together in some celebration and loose off some pent-up energy.
mt: Kiss es tan válido como un partido de fútbol americano; es una liberación, una oportunidad para que la gente olvide sus problemas por un par de horas, se reúna en una celebración y libere un poco de energía reprimida.

src: The owner, a bachelor in his fifties, slept on a camp-bed in the vestibule of the hostel and cooked on a two ring stove in the courtyard, mostly a kind of vegetable stew as far as I could make out.
mt: El dueño, un soltero de unos cincuenta años, dormía en una cama plegable en el vestíbulo del albergue y cocinaba en una estufa de dos fuegos en el patio, sobre todo un tipo de guiso de verduras, por lo que pude ver.

src: And I think it would actually need, if it's going to be taken on board, somebody appointed to look at this, or it be put in somebody's job brief and I would have thought ideally that it would be placed with the community worker, and I don't know quite how we're placed for community workers in East Oxford, but maybe it's something we could put to that department.
mt: Y creo que, de hecho, si se va a tener en cuenta, se necesitaría que alguien lo investigara o que se incluyera en el trabajo de alguien, y creo que, idealmente, debería asignárselo a un trabajador comunitario, y no sé muy bien cómo estamos situados con los trabajadores comunitarios en East Oxford, pero tal vez sea algo que podamos proponer a ese departamento.

src: Something at the core of him gave in to her.
mt: Algo en su esencia le cedió a ella.

src: When they got out they saw a Vauxhall Astra, its front badly damaged, directly behind in the inside lane.

src: They're not really severe at all, I mean, they're very much intended for, you know, the weekend er type of people, you know, somebody that just wants to get out on a Sunday afternoon with the children.

src: I only hope that the police committee will actually support those and get on towards introducing them, because that is a way of using some of the people in our village communities to erm, to keep an eye on the community and see that those people that shouldn't go round.
mt: Solo espero que el comité de policía realmente apoye esto y se empeñe en introducirlo, porque es una forma de utilizar a algunas personas de nuestras comunidades rurales para, eh, vigilar a la comunidad y asegurarse de que las personas que no deberían ir de un lado a otro no lo hagan.

src: Her fingers barely touched Sabine's in greeting, but one swift head-to-toe appraisal absorbed everything she had on, and dismissed it.

src: The very next day, Sunday, I would have to attend the same chapel in its religious function, and the fact that the previous evening it had been a battleground for people who were supposed to be friends and allies, while the ‘enemy’ got on with his job uninterrupted outside, led to a more or less permanent confusion in my mind, which I now believe to be totally justified, between violence and religion, and between fact and fancy [or film].
mt: Al día siguiente, domingo, tendría que asistir a la misma capilla en su función religiosa, y el hecho de que la noche anterior había sido un campo de batalla para personas que se suponían que eran amigos y aliados, mientras que el “enemigo” seguía con su trabajo ininterrumpido afuera, llevó a una confusión más o menos permanente en mi mente, que ahora creo que está totalmente justificada, entre la violencia y la religión, y entre los hechos y la fantasía [o la película].

src: Courageous in taking on the over-spending bogey that did such havoc in 1987, it nevertheless opened the floodgates to a fortnight of ‘we can give away more tax than you’, and exchanges between the two parties of a staggering triviality that effectively supplanted any wider debate about the economy.
mt: Aunque tuvo el valor de enfrentarse al fantasma del exceso de gastos que causó tanto daño en 1987, abrió las compuertas a una semana y media de “podemos regalar más impuestos que tú”, y a intercambios entre los dos partidos de una trivialidad asombrosa que suplantó de forma efectiva cualquier debate más amplio sobre la economía.

src: Manager Tommy Gilmour will fly to Puerto Rico in three weeks to try and bring the Camacho fight to Glasgow in January, giving Weir almost three months to get over his first ever cut in one hundred and sixty seven fights as both amateur and professional.
mt: El entrenador Tommy Gilmour viajará a Puerto Rico en tres semanas para intentar llevar la pelea de Camacho a Glasgow en enero, lo que le dará a Weir casi tres meses para recuperarse de su primera herida en ciento sesenta y siete peleas como aficionado y profesional.

src: ‘How are you and Niall getting on, by the way?’

src: Dr Barnardo's was another charity she took on at about the same time; this charity has left its old orphanage image behind, and the Princess feels her connections with it have been fruitful.
mt: La Dra. Barnardo fue otra organización benéfica que ella adoptó casi al mismo tiempo; esta organización benéfica ha dejado atrás su antigua imagen de orfanato, y la princesa siente que sus conexiones con ella han sido fructíferas.

src: In the cave itself, bas-reliefs sculpted close together on a stalagmite cone, hard to make out for the most part, except for an obvious and memorable reindeer some three feet long, in the museum, animals graphically carved or engraved on bone, many of them heads of horses, but fish too, and pieces of bone, antler and ivory carved quite elaborately into abstract patterns of diamond shapes, chevrons or spirals.
mt: Nella stessa grotta, i bassorilievi scolpiti uno accanto all'altro su un cono di stalagmite, per lo più difficili da distinguere, tranne che per una renna evidente e memorabile di circa un metro di lunghezza, nel museo, animali graficamente scolpiti o incisi sull'osso, molti dei quali teste di cavalli, ma anche pesci, e pezzi di osso, corna e avorio scolpiti in modo elaborato in modelli astratti di forme di diamante, chevron o spirali.

src: She parked outside the high brick wall rather than driving on to the forecourt, and as if he sensed her reluctance to enter the house again he didn't attempt to invite her inside — but neither did he make any attempt to get out of the car, and they sat in silence in the light from an overhead street-lamp.

src: In the meantime one studies them as landscapes, so to speak, simply to heighten one's pleasure in sight-seeing, to get behind the superficial appearances, to uncover the layers of the palimpsest and to see, for example, a piece of the tenth century in the way a street makes an abrupt turn or does something else unexpected.

mt: Se analizará el papel que desempeñan los grupos de presión en el sistema y se examinará el caso que se ha planteado en relación con la democracia, que se ve significativamente menoscabada por las “élites políticas”.

mt: Ahora la responsabilidad será de ellos y sería mejor que lo hicieran bien y que mostraran que han dejado atrás las discusiones y que ahora se llevan bien.

mt: Es por eso que es importante que te mezcles con la gente, que salgas y te desplace, porque si descubres que todos los demás están subiendo la colina más rápido que tú y todos los demás están cruzando las carreteras más rápido que tú y todo lo demás, eso debería decirte algo, ahora no quiero ni siquiera entrar en competencia, pero creo que es bueno de vez en cuando ver si puedes caminar tan rápido como ese chico de dieciocho años que está delante de ti.

mt: “Creo que eres horrible por decirme mentiras también, y fingir que cuando te cases te levantarás a las horribles... tan repugnantes... con ese hombre que parece un pez con ojos amarillentos fríos y piel verde amarillenta...”

src: They've got off to a good start with a pair of eighteen carat gold and turquoise earrings donated by the Elizabeth Gage company of London.
mt: Han tenido un buen comienzo con un par de pendientes de oro de dieciocho quilates y turquesa donados por la empresa Elizabeth Gage de Londres.

src: Yes, er er I mean I agree with you Chairman entirely, I mean the arguments have been made before and I will just say them again, that I think the District Council are quite remiss er in asking for this sum of money, particularly in view of the reason that the, the, the field is there, which is for the recreation of the people who live in the estate, and the back end of Southwell, and it always has been, er and that is a responsibility that has been taken on by this Parish Council, and if, if, if they feel that they, they wish to persist in asking an unreasonable price, and let's face it, the District Council is in a position to look at this situation and say, the Parish Council is doing an excellent job, we will sell them this land for a figure that they can afford.
mt: Sí, es decir, estoy totalmente de acuerdo con usted, señor presidente, es decir, los argumentos ya se han planteado antes y los volveré a repetir, que creo que el Ayuntamiento Distrital es bastante negligente al pedir esta suma de dinero, en particular, teniendo en cuenta el motivo por el que el campo está allí, que es para la recreación de las personas que viven en la finca y en el extremo sur de Southwell, y siempre lo ha sido, y es una responsabilidad que ha asumido este Ayuntamiento Parroquial, y si, si, si sienten que, que desean insistir en pedir un precio irrazonable, y seamos sinceros, el Ayuntamiento Distrital está en posición de mirar esta situación y decir, el Ayuntamiento Parroquial está haciendo un excelente trabajo, les venderemos esta tierra por una cantidad que puedan pagar.

src: Food took on better flavour, meals became enjoyable instead of a chore,’ she says, ‘If I break the diet, I know I haven't failed, but have a chance to learn from the experience.
mt: Il cibo ha assunto un sapore migliore, i pasti sono diventati piacevoli invece che una fatica”, dice, “Se infrango la dieta, so che non ho fallito, ma ho la possibilità di imparare dall’esperienza.

src: And Graham acknowledged: ‘He accepted what was said, and got on with the job.
mt: Y Graham reconoció: “Aceptó lo que se le dijo y siguió con el trabajo.

src: Our problem is we can't get at the buyers.
mt: Nuestro problema es que no podemos llegar a los compradores.

src: ‘That's not what I meant — of course you'll get over the rotten bastard.
mt: “No, no es eso lo que quería decir, por supuesto que superarás a ese bastardo.

src: Anyone who takes on public duties sets himself up for attack, and in Rees-Mogg's case, the succession of posts was dizzying.
mt: Cualquier persona que asuma funciones públicas se expone a ser atacada, y en el caso de Rees-Mogg, la sucesión de cargos fue vertiginosa.

src: But you see, what we've got is a set of fairly standard steps here which you got through no matter what the project and I hope that er, you've all had a bit of practice at that.
mt: Pero, vean, lo que tenemos aquí es un conjunto de pasos bastante estándar que se siguen independientemente del proyecto y espero que todos hayan tenido un poco de práctica con eso.

src: ‘Perhaps he thinks you'd get on,’ said Betty sarcastically, ‘— have a lot in common.’
mt: “Tal vez él piensa que os llevaréis bien”, dijo Betty sarcásticamente, “que tenéis mucho en común”.

src: ‘That's because Travis favours our father, while the rest of us take after our mother,’ Drew explained.
mt: “Es porque Travis se parece a nuestro padre, mientras que el resto de nosotros nos parecemos a nuestra madre”, explicó Drew.

src: ‘We got on really well.’
mt: “Nos llevamos muy bien”.

src: I would never take on an act who couldn't perform live.
mt: Nunca contrataría a un artista que no pudiera actuar en vivo.

src: When we had finally retrieved all his excess baggage, it took us even longer than in Mexico to clear customs because he insisted on unrolling a big holdall right there on the bench to get at his oilskins.
mt: Quando finalmente recuperammo tutti i suoi bagagli in eccesso, ci volle ancora più tempo che in Messico per passare la dogana perché insistette nel disfare una grande borsa proprio lì sulla panchina per recuperare i suoi impermeabili.

src: JE: Joan, were you at all reluctant to take on this major role so late in your career?
mt: JE: Joan, ¿te costó aceptarlo todo a estas alturas de tu carrera?

src: He didn't get out much.
mt: Él no salía mucho.

src: A major inquiry was launched immediately into how the bombers got through to plant the device in a hut used by police on security duty outside the Bishop Street courthouse.
mt: Se inició inmediatamente una investigación importante sobre cómo habían logrado los atacantes colocar el artefacto en una cabaña utilizada por la policía para tareas de seguridad fuera del tribunal de Bishop Street.

src: If there was trouble he'd have to spend the rest of the day putting it down and wouldn't be able to get on with the arms search at all.
mt: Si hubiera problemas, tendría que pasar el resto del día apaciguándolos y no podría continuar con la búsqueda de armas.

src: Now I'm not getting at you or Peter Underhill, both of being excellent fellows; but this is just the sort of thing I predicted would happen twenty years ago when we, as a country, started to ‘go metric’ and were assured that five years would see it done.
mt: Non sto attaccando né te né Peter Underhill, che sono entrambi eccellenti ragazzi; ma questo è esattamente il tipo di cosa che avevo predetto vent'anni fa quando noi, come paese, abbiamo iniziato a "passare al sistema metrico" e ci hanno assicurato che in cinque anni sarebbe stato fatto.

src: I 'ad a long stint wiv Galloway an' I was gutted when I got the push an' we 'ad ter get out o' the 'ouse.
mt: Pasé mucho tiempo con Galloway y me destrozó cuando me echaron y tuvimos que salir de la casa.

src: Jeff Hammond, of Willis Corroon, said the service would provide the means of dealing with these potential threats to businesses enabling them to get on with their main task — providing quality products to an ever more aware consumer market.
mt: Jeff Hammond, di Willis Corroon, ha affermato che il servizio fornirà i mezzi per far fronte a queste potenziali minacce alle imprese, consentendo loro di concentrarsi sul loro compito principale, ovvero fornire prodotti di qualità a un mercato dei consumatori sempre più consapevole.

src: Somehow we then got on to the theme of French poetry, and Eliot expressed surprise at one of Herbert Read's recent pronouncements on Laforgue and another nineteenth-century poet I cannot recall and about whom at the time I knew too little to be able to arrive at an opinion.
mt: De alguna manera, llegamos entonces al tema de la poesía francesa, y Eliot expresó su sorpresa por una de las recientes declaraciones de Herbert Read sobre Laforgue y otro poeta del siglo XIX que no puedo recordar y sobre el que en ese momento sabía demasiado poco como para poder llegar a una opinión.

src: The planning of operations by those who are going to carry them out, obviates delay and misunderstandings which happen to be caused by intermediate stages, and makes for the speed of execution which in any operation of this kind is an incalculable asset.
mt: La pianificazione delle operazioni da parte di coloro che le eseguiranno, evita ritardi e incomprensioni causati da fasi intermedie e consente una velocità di esecuzione che in qualsiasi operazione di questo tipo è un vantaggio incalcolabile.

src: We have to get off after three weeks.
mt: Tenemos que bajar después de tres semanas.

src: Mr Bell was dismissive of the criticisms, commenting: ‘I have managed to get through campaigns without knowing the names of my opponents and I think I will be reasonably successful this time, too.’
mt: El Sr. Bell hizo caso omiso de las críticas y comentó: “He logrado pasar campañas sin saber los nombres de mis oponentes y creo que esta vez también tendré un éxito razonable”.

src: One mustn't give in to pessimism, and it's pointless to look too far ahead, but in a couple of years she won't even be legal.
mt: Non bisogna cedere al pessimismo, e non ha senso guardare troppo avanti, ma tra un paio d'anni non sarà nemmeno legale.

src: If you could get on to Basil, then we can get the ball rolling.
mt: Si puedes hablar con Basil, entonces podemos poner en marcha las cosas.

src: Pandora, the first woman of Greek myth — as described by Hesiod — is made out of earth; at Zeus's command she was fashioned out of clay to be the instrument of divine retribution.
mt: Pandora, la prima donna del mito greco - come descritto da Esiodo - è fatta di terra; per ordine di Zeus è stata modellata con l'argilla per essere lo strumento della divina punizione.

src: This is also partly the reason why in Adorno's theory music in a sense takes on too much autonomy, so much as to create a danger of a relapse into idealism.
mt: Questo è anche in parte il motivo per cui nella teoria di Adorno la musica in un certo senso assume troppa autonomia, tanto da creare il pericolo di una ricaduta nell'idealismo.

src: I thought so I mean it was getting on a bit
mt: Lo pensavo, voglio dire, stava diventando un po' noioso

src: I haven't got time to plan it, I'd better get on with it.
mt: No tengo tiempo para planificar, mejor me encargo de ello.

src: He declared on television that "Poland's painful but necessary programme of getting out of economic catastrophe can only be realized with the understanding of the majority of the nation" and that "the country had made its choice" .
mt: Declaró en televisión que “el programa doloroso pero necesario de Polonia para salir de la catástrofe económica solo puede realizarse con el entendimiento de la mayoría de la nación” y que “el país había hecho su elección”.
